strife_mechanism:
FRINGE WORLD STRIFE: On arrival, roll D6. 4+ = Unstable (5+ for calmer setting).
Unstable worlds start at Instability +1.
Each campaign turn (Invasion step):
  +1D6 to Instability
  +1 per active Rival on this world
  -1 if you completed a Patron job this turn
  -1 if you Held the Field vs a Roving Threat this turn
If Instability >= 10: Roll D100 on Strife table, reduce by listed amount.

strife_events.name: Hooligans
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Hooligans. -5 Instability. A bunch of roided-up hooligans cause a riot. You cannot perform any Explore or Trade crew actions during the next campaign turn.

strife_events.name: Criminal Gang
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Criminal Gang. -5 Instability. Roll on Criminal Elements table (core rules p.94). This gang threatens local businesses. All post-battle payouts on this world are reduced by 1 credit until cleared. To clear: set up a Fight Off mission. Add 1 Specialist and an Enemy Boss Unique Individual. +1 Story Point if you kill the boss in Brawling combat.

strife_events.name: Enemy Infiltration
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Enemy Infiltration. -5 Instability. A Converted Infiltrator squad (core rules p.101) has arrived. Use the Track crew action (like tracking a Rival) to find them. Fight Off mission: enemy has +1 Combat Skill and 1 additional Specialist. Must check for Invasion every campaign turn until squad is destroyed.

strife_events.name: Heating Up
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Heating Up. -3 Instability. Tensions getting out of hand. Add a Rival randomly selected from the Criminal Elements subtable (core rules p.94).

strife_events.name: Sabotage
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Sabotage. -7 Instability. During a running gun battle, your ship was caught in crossfire. Ship takes 1D6+1 points of hull damage.

strife_events.name: Raiders
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Raiders. -7 Instability. Next campaign turn, attacked by scavengers. Resolve as Rival attack Raid scenario. Roll D6 for attackers: 1-2 Raiders, 3 Psychos, 4-5 Pirates, 6 Planetary Nomads. Add +2 to enemy numbers.

strife_events.name: Crackdown
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Crackdown. -10 Instability. Authorities making examples. Unless you leave next campaign turn, roll 3D6 in Upkeep step — discard every die showing 5 or 6, add remaining dice together and pay that many credits in fines, or have your ship confiscated.

strife_events.name: Economic Collapse
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Economic Collapse. -10 Instability. Cannot take Trade actions. All mission payouts -1 credit. Recovery: after each mission, roll D6 (+1 if Patron job, +4 if Quest). On 6+, economic malaise is over.

strife_events.name: Invasion Imminent
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Invasion Imminent! Instability no longer tracked. After the next campaign turn, the world is automatically invaded. Roll D6 for invaders: 1-2 Converted Acquisition, 3 Abductor Raiders, 4-5 Swarm Brood, 6 K'Erin Colonists.

strife_events.name: Civil War
strife_events.instruction: STRIFE: Civil War! Instability no longer tracked. World erupts in shooting war next turn. If you stay: roll twice on Interested Parties table (core rules p.99) — choose a side. Each mission is Opportunity vs opposing force, +2 regular +1 Specialist enemies, +1 credit per mission. Use Galactic War Progress (core rules p.126). Unity Victorious = 1D6 credits bonus + remove 1 Rival. Lost to Unity = ship + credits confiscated, booted off-world.

loan_origins.name: Unity Program
loan_origins.instruction: LOAN ORIGIN: Unity Program. Unity-funded org sponsors starship loans for economic development. Add +5 credits to loan amount for fees.

loan_origins.name: Sector Government Program
loan_origins.instruction: LOAN ORIGIN: Sector Government. Multi-system governments view private traders as valuable. Standard loan terms.

loan_origins.name: Corporate
loan_origins.instruction: LOAN ORIGIN: Corporate. Funding freelancers as investment (or tax write-off). Standard loan terms.

loan_origins.name: Free Trader
loan_origins.instruction: LOAN ORIGIN: Free Trader. Private loan from a trader who made it big. Add +1D6 credits to loan amount.

loan_origins.name: Suspicious Character
loan_origins.instruction: LOAN ORIGIN: Suspicious Character. Shady deal with questionable terms. Add +1D6 credits to loan amount.

interest_rates.name: Very Cheap
interest_rates.note: Free Trader treats this as Cheap instead.
interest_rates.instruction: INTEREST: Very Cheap. Low debt (<=30cr): +1/turn. High debt (>30cr): +1/turn. Note: Free Trader → Cheap.

interest_rates.name: Cheap
interest_rates.instruction: INTEREST: Cheap. Low debt (<=30cr): +1/turn. High debt (>30cr): +2/turn.

interest_rates.name: Average
interest_rates.instruction: INTEREST: Average. Low debt (<=30cr): +1/turn. High debt (>30cr): +3/turn.

interest_rates.name: Expensive
interest_rates.note: Suspicious Character treats this as Very Expensive instead.
interest_rates.instruction: INTEREST: Expensive. Low debt (<=30cr): +2/turn. High debt (>30cr): +3/turn. Note: Suspicious Character → Very Expensive.

interest_rates.name: Very Expensive
interest_rates.note: High interest = 1D6 rolled each turn. Unity Program treats this as Expensive instead.
interest_rates.instruction: INTEREST: Very Expensive. Low debt (<=30cr): +2/turn. High debt (>30cr): +1D6/turn. Note: Unity Program → Expensive.

enforcement_thresholds.name: Very Casual
enforcement_thresholds.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Very Casual. Threshold 1: 75 credits. Threshold 2: 90 credits.

enforcement_thresholds.name: Casual
enforcement_thresholds.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Casual. Threshold 1: 65 credits. Threshold 2: 80 credits.

enforcement_thresholds.name: Moderate
enforcement_thresholds.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Moderate. Threshold 1: 60 credits. Threshold 2: 75 credits.

enforcement_thresholds.name: Aggressive
enforcement_thresholds.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Aggressive. Threshold 1: 55 credits. Threshold 2: 70 credits.

enforcement_thresholds.name: Very Aggressive
enforcement_thresholds.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Very Aggressive. Threshold 1: 50 credits. Threshold 2: 65 credits.

enforcement_methods.name: Fees and Penalties
enforcement_methods.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Fees. A loan fee is added to the loan. May trigger further enforcement.

enforcement_methods.name: Collectors
enforcement_methods.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Collectors. Each Upkeep step, roll 1D6+1. If <= turns on current world, they catch you: fine of 3D6 credits. Can't pay = added to loan + Bounty Hunter Rival. Then remove Collectors. Can have multiple Collectors at once.

enforcement_methods.name: Collectors, Fringe-style
enforcement_methods.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Collectors, Fringe-style. Armed collectors added as Rival. Treated as normal Rival — won't stop even if you pay up. Must be dealt with. Can pay 10 credits to remove them from roster.

enforcement_methods.name: Temporary Seizure
enforcement_methods.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Temporary Seizure. Warrant for ship. Each turn roll 2D6: on 2-5, ship seized until debt reduced to 10 credits below seizure amount. Interest accrues unless Unity/Sector Government. Multiple rolls increase seizure range by 1 each time.

enforcement_methods.name: Permanent Seizure
enforcement_methods.instruction: ENFORCEMENT: Permanent Seizure. Warrant for ship. Each turn roll 2D6: on 2-6, ship permanently lost. Cancel by reducing debt to 10 credits below seizure amount. Multiple rolls increase range by 1.

fight_seizure_instruction: FIGHT SEIZURE: Set up battle vs Enforcers (core rules p.96), +2 basic enemies. Deploy center, 12"+ from edges. Enemy from 2 random edges, split evenly. Randomly determine your escape edge. If at least 1 crew escapes, you leave off-world. Warrant remains, Enforcer Rival added.
